context: Feet, ankles, obliques, shoulder girdle, alignment

corrections.text: Toes gripping instead of staying long and flat — noted in rises
Arches collapsing under load — noted in rises
Pelvic alignment improving but arches still compensating — general observation
Left ankle winging worse than right despite right being the injured side — noted in rises
Left single-leg relevé wobbles at the top of the rise — weakness drops off before reaching full height. Pause rises on left only, 2-count lower
Pinky toe must stay in contact with the floor throughout rise and lower
Match right plié depth on both sides — go only as far as right allows, left has been overloading to compensate
2/3 weight forward over the ball of the foot, 1/3 in the heel — established baseline for 'over your feet' in parallel
Shoulder blades creeping up, sitting into the traps — noted across all work
Épaulement initiates from the obliques and ribcage, not the shoulder pulling

homeExercises.name: Side plank
homeExercises.notes: Hips level, don't collapse forward. Left side active not passive

homeExercises.name: Side plank with arm to fifth
homeExercises.notes: Shoulder blade stays down as arm rises. Obliques initiate, not shoulder

homeExercises.name: Clamshell with hips lifted
homeExercises.notes: Pelvis stable, don't rock back as leg opens

homeExercises.name: Lying shoulder lift with twist to opposite hip
homeExercises.notes: Oblique initiates before shoulder moves — this is the épaulement feel

homeExercises.name: Slow Russian twists with ball, straight arms
homeExercises.notes: Ribcage rotates, shoulders down, no traps at end range

homeExercises.name: Ball pass around the back
homeExercises.notes: Shoulder blades stay down throughout, don't round the upper back to reach
